item.title: UK to host talks on plan to reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Yvette Cooper is due to convene a virtual meeting of foreign ministers, though the US is not expected to attend.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Gulf Nations and Israel Face New Attacks After Trump Claims Military Success
item.description: President Trump said the United States would hit Iran “extremely hard” over the next two to three weeks. He said the war was already a success but did not offer a concrete exit timeline.

item.title: Can Starmer steady the ship? Submit your questions
item.description: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with Stephen Bush, Miranda Green and Robert Shrimsley on Thursday April 2 at 3pm BST

item.title: Man dies in storm as Saharan dust shrouds Crete
item.description: A man dies in flooding near Athens as rain lashes several regions in Greece, while a Saharan dust storm enveloped Crete.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
Tories pledge to scrap carbon taxes on industry
item.description: Kemi Badenoch says carbon taxes have made doing business in Britain "much, much harder than it needs to be".

item.title: India’s central bank makes it harder to short the rupee
item.description: Currency rises after RBI blocks non-deliverable derivatives in the onshore market

item.title: Explosions at Burundi ammunition depot kill 13 civilians - army
item.description: Powerful blasts destroyed homes and sparked panic in the city of Bujumbura on Tuesday night.

item.title: New laws to make it easier to cancel subscriptions and get refunds
item.description: Consumers will be able to cancel unwanted subscriptions 'at the click of a button', the government said.

item.title: China’s Aiming for the Moon, and NASA Is Looking Over Its Shoulder
item.description: The U.S. space agency launched a lunar flyby Wednesday, but Beijing is pursuing its own space program with formidable focus. Here’s what we know about it, in photos and videos.

item.title: Buckling up for the stagflation trade
item.description: What can investors do?

item.title: The town that's lost faith in politics amid cost of living crisis
item.description: Shoppers are paying in cash to keep finances in check, not buying in bulk, and investing in higher-value items.

item.title: Trump’s threats to pull out of Nato leave alliance exposed
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Greek fraud investigation zeroes in on government members

item.title: A peek into who owes what and to whom
item.description: Striking a chord

item.title: FirstFT: Trump threatens to hit Iran ‘extremely hard’
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: conflict’s business winners and Gulf pipelines

item.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits off Indonesia, killing one
item.description: The quake struck between the islands of Manado and Ternate. Tsunami warnings have now been lifted.

item.title: Vulture funds circle private equity
item.description: Distressed-debt funds see a potential bonanza in the downturn in private credit

item.title: Italian leaders call for football chief’s head after World Cup humiliation
item.description: Azzurri fail to make third-straight men’s World Cup after humiliating loss to Bosnia and Herzegovina

item.title: World’s top energy traders wrongfooted in early days of Iran war
item.description: Oil traders that normally thrive on volatility caught out by scale of crisis as energy shock unfurled

item.title: Arms makers eye windfall as war drains stockpiles
item.description: Middle East conflict holds out promise to bring another windfall for defence groups as governments restock arsenals

item.title: Gulf states consider new pipelines to avoid Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Conflict prompts countries to revisit plans that replicate Saudi Arabia’s East-West pipeline despite huge cost and complexity

item.title: Poolside hunts data centre partners after CoreWeave deal falls through
item.description: AI start-up held talks with Google and other cloud providers in bid to revive ambitious 2-gigawatt Texas project

item.title: Ofwat poised to waive Thames Water fines to 2030 under deal
item.description: Clock is ticking on approving creditors’ emergency offer for utility as October deadline looms

item.title: The global wave of energy rationing
item.description: Governments from Bangladesh to Zambia impose measures to cut fuel demand as Middle East conflict cuts off flows

item.title: Trump’s protectionist Nixon redux takes a darker turn than the original
item.description: Tricky Dick’s trade and currency war gave way to a pivot towards the multilateral

item.title: UK bank capital rules are becoming a drag on growth
item.description: Reforming the capital regime would unlock meaningful lending capacity and strengthen Britain’s competitiveness

item.title: NHS staff boycott Palantir’s data platform over ethical concerns
item.description: Controversial US tech group was awarded a £330mn contract in 2023 to collate hospital and patient information

item.title: UK has ‘limited’ defences against ballistic missile threats, warns ex-RAF official
item.description: Former commander of Britain’s ground-based air defences also says the £1bn earmarked for investment is insufficient

item.title: How Apple fused Japanese craft with Chinese scale
item.description: Japanese industry and Chinese manufacturing prowess played roles in the tech giant’s success, with consequences for both

item.title: Why the Vatican is at war with Maga
item.description: Trump and Peter Thiel have managed to unite the disparate factions of the Catholic Church against an earthly enemy

item.title: 5 Takeaways From Trump’s Address on Iran
item.description: President Trump did not define a clear path out of the conflict, which he estimated would end within three weeks.

item.title: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
item.description: There were some glaring omissions in the president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

item.title: Trump says Iran war objectives 'nearing completion' in address to nation
item.description: Trump's first televised address of the conflict with Iran did little to quell uneasy markets.

item.title: Transcript: Trump’s Speech on Iran War
item.description: The president made his case for the U.S. attack, and said that the main objectives had been achieved.

item.title: Trump Initially Laid Out Five Goals for the Iran War. Here’s Where They Stand.
item.description: The United States and Israel have done significant damage to Iran’s military capabilities. But Iran still fires missiles, has nuclear material and coordinates with militias in the region.

item.title: U.S. Lifts Sanctions on Venezuela’s New Leader, Delcy Rodríguez
item.description: Removing sanctions would allow Delcy Rodríguez, the country’s acting leader, to conduct business with U.S. companies and potentially meet with President Trump.

item.title: Alleged Bondi gunman loses court bid to suppress names of his family
item.description: Lawyers for Naveed Akram had argued his mother, brother and sister live in "constant fear".

item.title: What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Wednesday.
item.description: Formidable strikes rocked Tehran early Wednesday morning. Iran launched what its state-aligned media called one of the largest attacks on Israel yet, damaging several cities.

item.title: In Speech, Trump Is Expected to Call Iran War a Success
Doctors lose new jobs package as strike to go ahead
item.description: The offer of 1,000 more training posts has been withdrawn after the union refused to scrap the planned six-day strike.

item.title: Israel's perpetual war with Iran may be hard to win with military might alone
item.description: The war with Iran has not yet resolved any of Israel's regional conflicts in the way its prime minister suggested it would.

item.title: Rachel Reeves says she's angry at Trump's decision to launch war with Iran
item.description: The chancellor says the US president does not have a "clear plan" to exit the conflict.

item.title: Israel’s Message to a Broad Swath of Lebanon: Shiites Must Go
item.description: Israel has issued sweeping evacuation warnings, and pressed some Christian and Druse leaders to expel Shiite Muslims from southern towns, the leaders said.

item.title: Evacuated From War-Torn Gaza, 11 Toddlers Return Home to Hugs and Tears
item.description: The Palestinian children were born prematurely in the early days of the war and moved from a besieged hospital to Egypt. They have now returned home to the enclave, which lies in ruins.

item.title: Rescuers abandon hope for whale stranded off German island
item.description: The whale became stranded on a sandbank before it was rescued, only to swim into shallow waters further along the coast.

item.title: When Racism Is a Crime: Brazil Puts a Tourist on Trial for Word and Gesture
item.description: An Argentine woman, who is white, could face years in prison after being accused of racism. The case has set off intense debate in Argentina and Brazil.

item.title: CPS giving 'investigative advice' to police over Andrew and Mandelson probes
item.description: Police are carrying out inquiries into Lord Mandelson and Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor's links to sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Captain Donnarumma vows to restore Italy after heartbreak
item.description: Italy captain Gianluigi Donnarumma says he "cried because of the enormous sadness" of not being able to lead the national side to the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Navy investigated links between nuclear sub commander and MP
item.description: No disciplinary action was taken over the allegations of an inappropriate relationship with Scottish MP Joani Reid.

item.title: Families cram into Greek court for trial into deadliest train crash
item.description: Fifty-seven people were killed when two trains collided in 2023 in the worst rail accident in Greek history, with 36 accused in a trial expected to last years.

item.title: UK will seek closer ties with EU in light of Iran war, Starmer says
item.description: It comes as UK-US relations have been strained by the PM's refusal to be dragged further into the Iran war.

item.title: Two migrants die in Channel crossing attempt
item.description: French authorities rescue eight people who get into difficulty boarding a small boat, but two of them died.

item.title: Try Living in Cape Town, Where 70% of Downtown Housing Is for Tourists
item.description: A shortage of affordable housing in the coastal city in South Africa has forced many people to live far outside the city center, while tourists occupy prime real estate.

item.title: French Prosecutors Link Foiled Attack on American Bank to a Pro-Iranian Group
item.description: The group is suspected of involvement in a string of attacks on Jewish targets in Belgium, Britain and the Netherlands.

item.title: DR Congo declares national holiday after reaching World Cup for first time in 52 years
item.description: This is just the second time that DR Congo have reached the World Cup finals, sparking celebrations in Kinshasa.

item.title: South African army arrive in crime hotspots to help tackle gangs
item.description: The army has been deployed to five of South Africa's nine provinces for a year-long operation.

item.title: SNP candidate ditched over handling of Linden complaints
item.description: Tracy Carragher was a deputy council leader when it was reported that her former colleague had made unwanted advances towards a teenager.

item.title: Minimum wage rises to £12.71 an hour as firms warn of impact
item.description: Many businesses have said they will have to pass higher wage costs onto customers.

item.title: Peter Magyar, the former Orban ally vying for power in Hungary
item.description: Magyar represents the biggest threat to Viktor Orban's rule in Hungary since he won the first of four consecutive victories in 2010.

item.title: Lamine Yamal condemns racist abuse aimed at Egypt
item.description: Lamine Yamal says racist chants heard during Spain's 0-0 draw with Egypt in Barcelona on Tuesday were "disrespectful and intolerable".

item.title: Analysis: Starmer holds off from emergency measures but warns storm is coming
item.description: The prime minister used a Downing Street news conference to make three big calls on the Iran war and cost of living pressures.

item.title: A final favour Emmanuel Macron could do for France
item.description: A gracefully timed exit would reset France’s awkward electoral calendar

item.title: On the front lines, Russian soldiers pay officers to stay alive
item.description: The war against Ukraine has created a corrupt economy of blood money

item.title: Will the European Union’s next member come from the north?
item.description: Worried about trade and security, Iceland and Norway are warming to the EU

item.title: A revolution is coming for Germany’s intelligence services
item.description: Derided at home and abroad, Germany’s spooks are about to get sweeping new powers

item.title: Senegal’s government denies the gravity of its debt crisis
item.description: Its refusal to consider an IMF plan may make things worse

item.title: Iran’s permissive party islands contain hidden dangers
item.description: If American troops turn up, they should not expect a package holiday

item.title: Iran has damaged a surprising amount of American kit
item.description: Losses would be much higher in a war with China

item.title: Iran’s opposition in exile is rethinking its support for the war
item.description: The longer the war drags on, the less clear are the benefits for ordinary Iranians

item.title: The war in Iran is nearing a crossroads
item.description: With a Trump-imposed deadline looming, a relative lull in the fighting cannot last much longer

item.title: How would American ground forces take Kharg?
item.description: Donald Trump says the war will end soon. But he is capable of feints

item.title: America’s foes see opportunity in Asia’s oil shock
item.description: Russia and China are quietly stepping in

item.title: Why women, more than men, are abandoning rural Japan
item.description: Bigger cities are, in turn, skewing more female

item.title: How the Gulf’s war is becoming Asia’s crisis too
item.description: Prices, debt, and scarcity will strike a blow against the world’s workshop

item.title: Mark Carney’s party is poised to take control of Parliament
item.description: Canada’s fate will probably soon be squarely in Liberal hands

item.title: Lessons for the world from tiny Hungary
item.description: A regime loved by MAGA may soon lose power. That matters

item.title: Devastated Italians reckon with 'third apocalypse' of World Cup failure
item.description: Azzurri fans and management alike are pondering what went wrong after Italy fail to qualify for third World Cup in a row.

item.title: How China hopes to win from the war
item.description: Never interrupt your enemy when he is making a mistake

item.title: Why the Iran war hurts China less than its rivals
item.description: But more than it admits

item.title: Brazil judge blocks Sugarloaf Mountain zipline
item.description: The project to build a zipline linking the popular Rio tourist spot to Morro da Urca proved controversial.

item.title: How worried should you be about private credit?
item.description: Its humbling could raise borrowing costs

item.title: The Fall of a Cambodian Money-Laundering Giant
item.description: The former chairman of a financial group was brought to China in handcuffs, the latest high-level capture in a widening investigation into organized crime.

item.title: Iran war economic shocks will last 'months', says Australia's PM
item.description: Anthony Albanese used a rare TV address to tell Australians that the "months ahead may not be easy" amid the global effects of the war.

item.title: Israel intensifies Lebanon attacks and hits areas not in Hezbollah's control
item.description: Attacks have continued as Israel has announced its intention to control swathes of south Lebanon.

item.title: U.S. Plans Military Expansion in Greenland
item.description: The Pentagon is in talks with Denmark for access to three more areas on the Arctic island. Several Greenlanders said they didn’t like the idea.

item.title: Senegal president signs tough new anti-LGBT law doubling jail terms
item.description: The new law imposes a maximum penalty of 10 years in jail and criminalises the "promotion" of same-sex relations.

item.title: Stanford University wins battle to keep diaries of Mao Zedong's secretary
item.description: Stanford said the papers, which mentioned the Tiananmen Massacre, would be banned if returned to Beijing.

item.title: 29 Killed in Russian Military Plane Crash in Crimea
item.description: Investigators cited a possible technical malfunction.

item.title: Energy bill help would be based on household income, Reeves says
item.description: The chancellor tells the BBC it is "too early" to say exactly who would get help but hinted any support would not arrive until the autumn.

item.title: China is trying to play peacemaker in the Iran war - will it work?
item.description: It's a turnaround for Beijing, whose official response has been muted so far. Why is China stepping in now?

item.title: 'Felt close to death': Indian seafarers detained in Iran return home
item.description: The crew members, whose ship was seized in December, got their release order a day before the war began.

item.title: Divorced couples in Japan can now share custody of their children
item.description: Japan was, until recently, the only G7 country to not recognise the legal concept of joint custody.

item.title: The Sailors Stranded in the Persian Gulf
item.description: Thousands of civilian sailors have been stranded for more than a month in waters surrounded by a conflict zone because of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: In UK Prisons, Drones Fly In Contraband ‘As if by Uber Eats’
item.description: Decades-old prison buildings were designed to be secure from the ground but not the air. Experts say that makes a lucrative smuggling trade hard to tackle.

item.title: 'My six-year-old has nosebleeds': Chiang Mai air pollution sparks health fears
item.description: It's peak fire season in the Thai city, which now ranks as one of the world's most polluted.

item.title: Tuanzebe sends DR Congo back to World Cup after 52 years
item.description: DR Congo qualify for their first World Cup since 1974 as Axel Tuanzebe's strike gives the Leopards a 1-0 win over Jamaica in their play-off.

item.title: 'You're no longer my sister' - rows erupt as war divides Iranian families
item.description: People in Iran describe angry scenes and tense relationships as rifts open up over the war.

item.title: 'A million things could go wrong' - why seizing Iran's uranium would be so risky for the US
item.description: Seizing the stockpile would be one of the "most complicated special operations in history", a former defence official tells the BBC.

item.title: Asia's migrant workers debate if Gulf jobs are worth deadly risk of Iran war
item.description: Iran's strikes on Gulf states have been especially hard for migrants who have long supported these economies to lift their families back home from poverty.

item.title: Peacekeepers killed by roadside explosion in Lebanon, initial report finds
item.description: The deaths of the two Indonesian nationals came less than a day after another UN peacekeeper - also Indonesian - was killed in Lebanon.

item.title: UK and France extend talks over new small boats deal
item.description: A three-year deal to pay for more French patrols to intercept smuggling gangs was due to expire at midnight.

item.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
item.description: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

item.title: King's state visit to US to go ahead in April, but no Harry meeting
item.description: Despite political tensions between the US and UK, the King will travel to Washington next month.

item.title: More UK troops to be sent to Middle East, defence secretary announces
item.description: The UK government announces further air defence systems will be sent to countries in the region along with troops to operate them.

item.title: Eritrea advance after 19-year absence from Afcon qualifying
item.description: Eritrea are one of six nations to reach the group stage of qualification for Afcon 2027, having played their first games in the competition since 2007.

item.title: Judge rules Trump unlawfully terminated legal status of migrants who used US entry app
item.description: Thousands of people who entered the US through a Biden-era parole programme using the CBP One entry app will have their status restored.

item.title: The perils of a ground war in Iran
item.description: Donald Trump may send in troops. Does he know what to do with them?

item.title: Italian Christmas meal tragedy turns into murder inquiry
item.description: Tests results suggest the two women may have been killed with ricin, according to Italian media, but police still do not have a suspect.

item.title: Index providers should not bend the rules for Elon Musk
item.description: They will only expose ordinary investors to unnecessary risks

item.title: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
item.description: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

item.title: Three Reform candidates quit in one Welsh constituency
item.description: It is unclear who is standing for Reform in the constituency for the Vale of Glamorgan and Bridgend.

item.title: Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks face death penalty under new Israeli law
item.description: The new law, passed on Monday, was pushed hard by the far-right and Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

item.title: Is China covering up a violent attack at a Beijing market?
item.description: News outlets put a premium on making the country seem harmonious

item.title: Putin’s Internet Blackout: A Chaotic Drive to Cut Off Russians From the World
item.description: With new outages and blockages, President Vladimir V. Putin is taking his boldest steps yet to control Russians’ communications.

item.title: Labour suspends outspoken backbench MP Karl Turner
item.description: The Hull East MP says he was suspended because of his "robust" criticism of the government's policy on jury trials.

item.title: Green candidate replaced by rival who complained about him
item.description: Guy Ingerson was due to top the North East list for the party until he was dropped due to an unresolved complaint.

item.title: Nigeria warns its citizens in South Africa to be cautious after march turns violent
item.description: Solomon Ogbonna Eziko's installation as an Igbo leader in East London has sparked tensions.

item.title: Reform UK vows to scrap tax for short-haul family flights
item.description: The party says the move would "lighten the load" on families amid wider pressures on living costs.

item.title: Wolf bites woman in shock German attack on busy Hamburg shopping street
item.description: It is believed to be the first wolf attack on a human since the animals began to reestablish themselves in Germany decades ago.

item.title: Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say
item.description: An 11-year-old is reported to have been killed in an air strike while manning a checkpoint in Tehran.

item.title: Russian oil tanker docks in Cuba ending near-total blockade
item.description: A Russian oil tanker has docked in Cuba, providing temporary relief after months of shortages.

item.title: At least 16 killed and thousands displaced by gang attack in rural Haiti
item.description: The attack happened in the Artibonite region, where members of the Gran Grif gang have targeted locals in the past.

item.title: More than 70 miners killed in South Sudan as government and opposition trade blame
item.description: Both government and opposition groups have been accused of being responsible for the deadly attack.

item.title: Huge fires at Russian oil facilities following Ukraine strikes, satellite images show
item.description: BBC Verify examines recent damage to three key Russian oil export facilities near the Baltic Sea.

item.title: Zelensky says allies asked him to scale back attacks on Russian energy
item.description: Ukraine's president says partners asked if the attacks could be "reduced" as prices soar due to the Iran war.

item.title: Ghana sack manager Addo, 72 days before World Cup
item.description: Ghana dismiss manager Otto Addo, 72 days before the start of the 2026 World Cup, following a run of defeats.

item.title: Shock, sadness and relief in town at centre of Australia's seven-month police manhunt
item.description: Porepunkah has been in the spotlight since Dezi Freeman shot dead two police officers last year.

item.title: 'A succulent Chinese meal' - iconic Australian quote immortalised in national film archive
item.description: The theatrical monologue given by Jack Karlson as he was arrested in 1991 has been officially recognised as a "lasting piece of cultural folklore".

item.title: How Pakistan won over Trump to become an unlikely mediator in the Iran war
item.description: Pakistan's role as intermediary in this conflict took many by surprise. But perhaps it shouldn't have.

item.title: Social media firms must better enforce Australia under-16 ban, watchdog says
item.description: Regulator eSafety says it has concerns about how Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube are complying with the ban.

item.title: Mexico demands answers after another migrant dies in ICE custody
item.description: President Sheinbaum vows to take action after the death of a Mexican last week at a California facility.

item.title: Binyamin Netanyahu is down—but not out
item.description: Ahead of an election due in October, a divided opposition and the war could hand him a lifeline

item.title: Gazan mother reunited with evacuated daughter after two years
item.description: The girl is among at least eight children who were premature newborns when they were evacuated during heavy fighting in Gaza at the start of the war.

item.title: Most Syrian refugees in Germany expected to return home in three years, Merz says
item.description: Merz’s government has taken a tougher line on refugees and migrants, amid surging support for the anti-immigration AfD party.

item.title: Céline Dion announces comeback shows: 'I'm so ready for this'
item.description: The star was diagnosed with a rare condition that affects her singing voice and her ability to walk.

item.title: For China’s officials, the goal was once growth. Now it’s loyalty
item.description: An obsession with GDP is replaced by an obsession with Xi Jinping

item.title: Oil rises and Asia shares slide as Iran war enters fifth week
item.description: It comes after Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen joined the conflict by striking Israel over the weekend.

item.title: How the US could try to seize Iran's Kharg Island
item.description: Donald Trump has indicated he could send troops to take control of Iran's key oil export terminal.

item.title: Latin Patriarch will have access to Jerusalem holy site after police stopped entry
item.description: The Israeli PM says Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa had been asked not to enter the church over concerns about Iranian strikes.

item.title: The Venezuela Donald Trump “runs” is a land of surreal contrasts
item.description: Animal spirits are stirring. The opposition has hope. The regime’s softening of repression is uneven

item.title: Korir banned for five years over doping admission
item.description: Former New York marathon winner Albert Korir of Kenya is banned for five years after admitting to doping.

item.title: Rationing power and diluting petrol - how African countries are coping with effects of Iran war
item.description: The disruption in oil supplies has caused shortages and concerns in several African countries.

item.title: Three Lebanese journalists killed in Israeli strike, say broadcasters
item.description: The Israeli military confirms it killed Ali Shoeib and Mohamed Ftouni, alleging they also belonged to Hezbollah's military wing.
